regime_justification: USDJPY grinding higher toward 159 (6 straight sessions of yen weakness), JGB 10Y at 2.78% (highest since 1997), 30Y JGB at record highs, MOVE spiking +14.7%, Iran conflict pushing oil to $103+, BOJ board member Masu urging rate hikes 'as soon as possible', MOF intervention constraints tightening per IMF rules. Carry trade under pressure but no sharp unwind yet.

domino_stage.label: Pre-Intervention
domino_stage.justification: USDJPY at 158.88 (within 1.12 yen of 160 watch line), MOF already used one of three allowed intervention windows per IMF rules (early May), JGB yields spiking (10Y 2.78%, 30Y at record 4.19%), MOVE index +14.7% in one day signaling bond vol regime shift, BOJ board split 6-3 at April meeting with three dissenting for immediate hike, oil shock driving imported inflation.

1w.label: Watchful
1w.driver: USDJPY pace elevated (6 consecutive sessions weaker), JGB yields at multi-decade highs, MOVE spiking, BOJ next meeting Jun 13-14 (outside 1w window), MOF intervention ammunition limited (2 windows left by Nov per IMF)

2w.label: Watchful
2w.driver: G7 meeting this coming week (May 18-19 per MOF statement) raises FX coordination risk, BOJ June meeting on radar, oil shock persistent (Hormuz closed), JGB 10Y above 2.7% is unwind catalyst threshold per historical precedent

3w.label: Watchful
3w.driver: BOJ June meeting (Jun 13-14) inside window - split vote in April (6-3) suggests hawkish minority gaining traction, inflation forecast raised to 2.8% for FY2026, Masu (board member) calling for hikes 'without hesitation'

4w.label: Watchful
4w.driver: Post-BOJ June decision positioning, Iran conflict resolution uncertainty, fiscal stress indicators (Japan debt-to-GDP 250%, 30Y JGB at record) suggesting structural pressure on carry trade viability

bottom_line: Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention): USDJPY at 158.88, JGB 10Y at 2.78% (highest since 1997), 30Y at record 4.19%. MOF has two intervention windows left through November per IMF rules. BOJ board split 6-3 at April meeting with member Masu urging rate hikes 'as soon as possible'. MOVE index surged 14.7% Friday signaling rates vol regime change. Oil at $103 (Iran conflict), ¥/bbl at ¥16,417 driving imported inflation. US-JP spread 187 bps but compressing as JGBs sell off. Nikkei down 3 days (-0.97% today to 60,816) on fiscal stress fears. Carry-to-vol deteriorating but no forced unwind yet. Grades C across all horizons: setup is fragile, MOF running out of tools, BOJ June meeting (Jun 13-14) and G7 this week are key catalysts. Applied unwind prior to 3w/4w grades; data-driven grades would be D but BOJ hawkish minority and fiscal stress tipping scales to C.

stack.note: 6th consecutive session weaker; 1.12 yen from 160 MOF watch line; 5d pace +0.7% elevated but below intervention threshold (~2%)
Highest since May 1997; spiked on Iran war inflation fears, fiscal stress (debt-to-GDP 250%), BOJ QT; 30Y at record 4.19%
Touched 4.63% intraday Monday (highest since Jan 2025); Fed hike pricing rising (~50% chance by Dec per reports)
Spread compressing as JGBs sell off faster than USTs; down from ~200 bps last week; carry less attractive but still positive
Jumped from 69.63 Friday; rates vol regime shift; bond market repricing Iran inflation shock; approaching 100 stress threshold
Up from 17.26 Friday; modest elevation on Iran fears and equity selloff; still below 20 'fear' threshold
Iran conflict; Trump warned 'clock ticking'; Hormuz effectively closed; touched $108 intraday; up 66% YoY
Japan import cost pressure; WTI 103.24 × 158.88; driving BOJ inflation forecast to 2.8% FY2026

trigger_map.note: USDJPY 158.88, 1.12 yen from 160. MOF has 2 of 3 intervention windows left through Nov per IMF rules (used one early May). G7 meeting this week raises coordination risk. Katayama met Bessent May 12, discussed FX.

April meeting split 6-3 (Takata, Tamura, Nakagawa dissented for 1.0%). Board member Masu called for hikes 'as soon as possible' and 'without hesitation' if inflation risks intensify. Next meeting Jun 13-14. FY2026 CPI forecast raised to 2.8%.

trigger_map.trigger: JGB 10Y break above prior cycle high
trigger_map.note: 10Y at 2.78%, highest since May 1997 (prior cycle high ~2.0% in 2018). 30Y at record 4.19%. Bond market repricing fiscal risk (250% debt-to-GDP) and Iran inflation shock. BOJ selling off holdings (QT).
MOVE at 79.87 (+14.7% Friday), up from 69.63. Approaching 100 stress threshold. Rates vol regime shifting on Iran war, Fed hike pricing, JGB selloff. Bond vigilantes waking up.

trigger_map.trigger: Energy / Hormuz shock pushing ¥/bbl > prior peak
trigger_map.note: ¥/bbl at ¥16,417 (WTI $103 × 158.88). Hormuz effectively closed, Trump threatened Iran 'clock ticking'. Oil up 66% YoY. BOJ raised FY2026 inflation to 2.8% on energy shock. Import-cost spiral underway.

narrative.p1: USDJPY ground 11 bps higher to 158.88 in its sixth consecutive session of yen weakness, now just 1.12 yen (112 pips) below the 160 MOF watch line. JGBs sold off violently: 10Y spiked 13 bps to 2.78% (highest since May 1997), 30Y hit a record 4.19%. The MOVE index jumped 14.7% Friday to 79.87, signaling a rates volatility regime shift as bond markets repriced Iran war inflation risk and Fed hike odds (~50% chance by Dec). Oil held above $103 after Trump warned Iran the 'clock is ticking', with Hormuz still effectively closed. Nikkei fell 0.97% (third straight day) to 60,816 on fiscal stress fears—Japan's 250% debt-to-GDP ratio now colliding with surging bond yields. BOJ board member Masu reiterated calls for rate hikes 'as soon as possible', echoing the April meeting's 6-3 split vote where three members dissented for an immediate hike to 1.0%. MOF Finance Minister Katayama met US Treasury Secretary Bessent May 11-12 to discuss FX markets ahead of this week's G7 meeting. Stage upgraded from 1 (Pressure Building) to 2 (Pre-Intervention) as USDJPY approaches watch line, intervention ammunition dwindles (2 of 3 IMF-allowed windows left through Nov), and bond yields break multi-decade resistance.
narrative.p2: Quant anchors: US-JP 10Y spread at 181 bps, down 14 bps as JGBs sell off faster than USTs—spread compressing but still positive. Carry-to-vol proxy deteriorating (spread/vol unavailable but directionally negative as vol spikes). ¥/bbl at ¥16,417 (+66% YoY), driving BOJ's FY2026 inflation forecast to 2.8% from 1.9%. USDJPY 5-day change +0.7%—elevated but below the ~2% pace that historically triggers MOF action. Distance from 60-day mean: stretched (USDJPY rallied from ~155 in early May). MOVE at 79.87 approaching the 100 threshold that marks rates-vol regime change and carry liquidation risk. We are now at Stage 2 (Pre-Intervention): USDJPY at watch line, MOF running low on intervention tools, JGB yields breaking out, and BOJ hawkish minority gaining voice. Applied unwind prior to 2w/3w/4w grades: data-driven would be D (spreads positive, no sharp moves yet) but the setup is fragile—BOJ June meeting (Jun 13-14), G7 this week, MOF's limited ammunition, and fiscal stress tipping scales to C. 1w grade C on elevated pace and bond vol spike; 2w C on G7 coordination risk and BOJ meeting approaching; 3w/4w C on June BOJ decision and structural fiscal/inflation pressures. Without the prior, 3w/4w would grade D.
narrative.p3: BTC at $77,319 (-1.4% today, -5.2% over 7d), down 25% from its October 2025 all-time high of $126k. Crypto is draining on the Iran conflict (Trump strike threats over the weekend), broad risk-off, and dollar strength (DXY +0.49% today). XRP at $1.39 (-1.7% 24h), tracking BTC's weakness. The selloff reflects the carry unwind thesis playing out in risk assets: as yen strengthens (even modestly) and rates vol spikes, levered longs in high-beta assets like crypto face funding pressure. BTC open interest direction unavailable but 24h volume elevated ($30B+ per sources), suggesting liquidation flow rather than accumulation. Crypto positioning takeaway: the liquidity bid that lifted BTC to $126k in Oct 2025 is reversing as the yen carry trade comes under pressure. If USDJPY breaks below 155 (MOF intervention or BOJ hike), expect accelerated crypto drain as carry unwind cascades across risk assets. For the operator's long-term crypto position: risk is rising but no forced deleveraging yet—monitor 1) USDJPY break below 157 (signal of carry capitulation), 2) BTC break below $75k (Oct 2024 support), and 3) BOJ June 13-14 decision. Crypto remains sympathy play to macro liquidity; yen strength = crypto weakness.

horizon_rationale.w1_w2: 1w grade C: USDJPY pace elevated (6 sessions up, +0.7% over 5d), JGB 10Y at 2.78% (highest since 1997), MOVE spiking to 79.87 signals bond vol regime shift, but no imminent catalyst inside 7 days (BOJ June meeting Jun 13-14 is outside window, MOF likely waiting for G7 cover). 2w grade C: G7 Finance Ministers meeting this week (May 18-19 in Paris per MOF) raises FX coordination risk—MOF could use G7 statement as political cover for intervention if USDJPY pushes above 160. BOJ June meeting moves into 2w forward window. Applied unwind prior: data-driven 1w would be D (no imminent catalyst) but bond vol spike and MOF ammunition constraints tip to C.
horizon_rationale.w2_w3: 2w grade C holds: G7 meeting outcome unclear, BOJ June meeting (Jun 13-14) moves into 3w window. 3w grade C: BOJ decision is key catalyst. April meeting split 6-3 with three members dissenting for immediate hike to 1.0%; board member Masu called for hikes 'as soon as possible'. FY2026 inflation forecast raised to 2.8% on Iran oil shock. Even if BOJ holds at 0.75%, hawkish guidance or another split vote could trigger yen short covering. If BOJ hikes to 1.0%, USDJPY likely breaks below 155 and triggers Stage 3-4 unwind. Applied unwind prior here: data-driven would be D (BOJ consensus is hold) but hawkish minority gaining traction and inflation overshoot justifies C.
horizon_rationale.w3_w4: 3w grade C, 4w grade C: post-BOJ positioning and Iran conflict resolution path dominate. If BOJ hikes in June, 4w window is unwind/stabilization phase (Stage 4-5). If BOJ holds, carry trade limps along but structural pressures remain: JGB yields at multi-decade highs, fiscal stress (250% debt-to-GDP), oil shock persistent. MOF has only 2 intervention windows left through November per IMF rules—running out of tools. 4w scenario is either forced unwind (if BOJ hikes) or fragile stability (if BOJ holds but yields keep rising). Applied unwind prior to 4w: data-driven would be D (if BOJ holds, status quo extends) but structural fiscal/inflation risks and limited policy tools justify C. Grades non-monotonic (C/C/C/C) because catalyst risk is distributed rather than concentrated—no single event dominates but multiple triggers (MOF intervention, BOJ June meeting, Iran war, JGB breakout) all cluster in 1-4w window.

watch_list: USDJPY daily closes above 159.50 (signals MOF tolerance rising or intervention ineffective)
JGB 10Y sustained close above 2.80% (fiscal crisis threshold; prior cycle high was ~2.0% in 2018)
MOVE index break above 100 (rates vol regime change triggers cross-asset carry unwind)
BOJ June 13-14 meeting statement and vote split (3+ dissenters or hawkish guidance = yen spike)
G7 Finance Ministers statement on FX this week (May 18-19 Paris) - coordinated intervention language or MOF green light
Iran-US conflict resolution or escalation headlines (Hormuz reopening = oil relief; new strikes = further shock)
WTI sustained close above $110 (¥/bbl > ¥17,000 accelerates BOJ inflation overshoot)
